1	/**
2	 * Lesson Duplication Orchestrator
3	 *
4	 * Drives the duplication pipeline for a single LessonDuplications record:
5	 *  1. K2: Select up to 20 exercises from source lesson (via selectExercisesScaled)
6	 *  2. For each exercise, concurrently (max 3):
7	 *     a. Route to strategy (script or AI) → generates new exercise content
8	 *     b. Run structural validator on the generated content
9	 *     c. If structural passes AND strategy != 'script', run semantic validator
10	 *     d. On any failure, append a failure entry to the LessonDuplications record
11	 *  3. After all exercises settle: set status=succeeded (0 failures) or needs_review (>0 failures)
12	 *
13	 * Failures are streamed live — each failure is written to the DB immediately
14	 * via payload.update(), not accumulated in memory.
15	 */
16	
17	import type { Payload } from 'payload'
18	import type { Exercise } from '@/payload-types'
19	import type { ContentBlock } from '@/server/payload/collections/Exercises/schemas'
20	import type { DuplicationSubject } from '@/server/payload/collections/LessonDuplications'
21	import { logger } from '@/infra/utils/logger'
22	import { selectExercisesScaled } from '@/server/services/lesson-duplication/selectors'
23	import { getSourceExercisesForLesson } from '@/server/services/lesson-duplication/source-exercises'
24	import {
25	  validateExerciseStructural,
26	  fillMissingFieldsWithPlaceholders,
27	  BLOCKING_FAILURE_CODES,
28	  type StructuralFailure,
29	} from '@/server/services/lesson-duplication/validators/structural'
30	import {
31	  validateExerciseSemantic,
32	  SEMANTIC_FAILURE_CODE,
33	} from '@/server/services/lesson-duplication/validators/semantic'
34	import { RouterStrategy } from '@/server/services/lesson-duplication/strategies/router'
35	
36	// Concurrency of 1 = process exercises sequentially. Each exercise hits the
37	// LLM twice (creative + deterministic). Gemini's per-minute quota is easily
38	// exceeded at higher concurrency, which triggered "rate limit exceeded"
39	// failures on every exercise in early multi-exercise runs. The variation
40	// service has rate-limit backoff as a safety net, but serializing is the
41	// primary control. Bump this back up only after we've sized the quota AND
42	// replaced appendEntry's read-modify-write with an atomic $push.
43	// `as const` pins the literal type so the compile-time guard below catches
44	// any bump (current value preserved as literal `1`).
45	export const CONCURRENCY_LIMIT = 1 as const
46	
47	export const GENERATION_FAILURE_CODE = 'GENERATION_FAILED' as const
48	
49	export type DuplicationStrategy = 'script' | 'ai'
50	
51	/** Result of running the strategy for one exercise. */
52	export interface StrategyResult {
53	  exerciseId: string
54	  strategy: DuplicationStrategy
55	  blocks: ContentBlock[]
56	}
57	
58	/**
59	 * Generate a new exercise via the appropriate strategy.
60	 *
61	 * K3: ScriptVariationStrategy for purely-algebraic exercises at light level.
62	 * K4: AiVariationStrategy for all other cases (throws until AI is wired up).
63	 *
64	 * @param exercise    Source exercise to vary
65	 * @param level       Duplication level (none/light/medium/deep)
66	 * @param _payload    Payload instance (available for future AI strategy use)
67	 * @returns           StrategyResult with generated blocks and strategy used
68	 */
69	export async function runStrategy(
70	  exercise: ExerciseDoc,
71	  level: 'none' | 'light' | 'medium' | 'deep',
72	  subject: DuplicationSubject,
73	  payload: Payload,
74	  router?: RouterStrategy,
75	): Promise<StrategyResult> {
76	  const activeRouter = router ?? new RouterStrategy(payload)
77	  const result = await activeRouter.apply(exercise as unknown as Exercise, level, subject)
78	
79	  const content = result.exercise.content as unknown as { blocks: ContentBlock[] }
80	  return {
81	    exerciseId: exercise.id,
82	    strategy: result.needsAiFallback ? 'ai' : 'script',
83	    blocks: content.blocks ?? [],
84	  }
85	}
86	
87	/** Suggested action for a failure, based on failure code. */
88	function suggestAction(code: string): 'skip' | 'regenerate' | 'keep' {
89	  switch (code) {
90	    case 'MISSING_QUESTION':
91	    case 'MISSING_HINT':
92	    case 'MISSING_SOLUTION':
93	    case 'MISSING_FULL_SOLUTION':
94	    case 'MISSING_CORRECT_OPTION':
95	    case 'MISSING_WRONG_OPTIONS':
96	    case 'INVALID_SVG':
97	    case 'PNG_FORBIDDEN':
98	    case 'TOO_MANY_SECTIONS':
99	    case 'INVALID_GEOMETRY_SPEC':
100	    case 'INVALID_AXIS_SPEC':
101	    case 'INVALID_GUIDED_EXPLANATION':
102	      return 'regenerate'
103	    case 'SEMANTIC_MISMATCH':
104	      return 'regenerate'
105	    case 'GENERATION_FAILED':
106	      return 'skip'
107	    default:
108	      return 'skip'
109	  }
110	}
111	
112	// Compile-time guard: this whole module assumes CONCURRENCY_LIMIT === 1.
113	// appendEntry below uses a non-atomic read-then-update on the failures/warnings
114	// arrays — at concurrency >1 two parallel appends would race and lose entries.
115	// If you raise CONCURRENCY_LIMIT, replace appendEntry with a Mongo $push update
116	// before deleting this assert.
117	type _AssertConcurrencyOne = typeof CONCURRENCY_LIMIT extends 1 ? true : never
118	const _concurrencyAssert: _AssertConcurrencyOne = true
119	void _concurrencyAssert
120	
121	/**
122	 * Append a single failure/warning entry to the LessonDuplications record.
123	 *
124	 * The bucket name is chosen by the caller:
125	 *  - 'failures' = blocking; the exercise was dropped from the output lesson.
126	 *  - 'warnings' = non-blocking; the exercise was kept with TODO placeholders
127	 *    for the missing field. Admin polishes via the review screen.
128	 */
129	async function appendEntry(
130	  bucket: 'failures' | 'warnings',
131	  payload: Payload,
132	  duplicationId: string,
133	  exerciseRef: string,
134	  sectionIndex: number,
135	  code: string,
136	  message: string,
137	): Promise<void> {
138	  const action = suggestAction(code)
139	  try {
140	    const current = await payload.findByID({
141	      collection: 'lesson-duplications',
142	      id: duplicationId,
143	      depth: 0,
144	      overrideAccess: true,
145	    })
146	    await payload.update({
147	      collection: 'lesson-duplications',
148	      id: duplicationId,
149	      data: {
150	        [bucket]: [
151	          // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any
152	          ...(((current as any)[bucket] as any[]) ?? []),
153	          {
154	            exerciseRef,
155	            sectionIndex,
156	            code,
157	            message,
158	            suggestedAction: action,
159	            resolved: false,
160	          },
161	        ],
162	      } as never,
163	      overrideAccess: true,
164	    })
165	  } catch (err) {
166	    logger.error(
167	      { err, duplicationId, exerciseRef, code, bucket },
168	      `Failed to append ${bucket} entry to LessonDuplications`,
169	    )
170	    // Don't rethrow — streaming failure should not abort the run
171	  }
172	}
173	
174	/** Append a blocking failure (exercise dropped from output lesson). */
175	async function appendFailure(
176	  payload: Payload,
177	  duplicationId: string,
178	  exerciseRef: string,
179	  sectionIndex: number,
180	  code: string,
181	  message: string,
182	): Promise<void> {
183	  return appendEntry('failures', payload, duplicationId, exerciseRef, sectionIndex, code, message)
184	}
185	
186	/** Append a non-blocking warning (exercise kept with placeholder). */
187	async function appendWarning(
188	  payload: Payload,
189	  duplicationId: string,
190	  exerciseRef: string,
191	  sectionIndex: number,
192	  code: string,
193	  message: string,
194	): Promise<void> {
195	  return appendEntry('warnings', payload, duplicationId, exerciseRef, sectionIndex, code, message)
196	}
197	
198	/**
199	 * Append a successful output-exercise mapping to the LessonDuplications
200	 * record. Used to stream progress so a Vercel function timeout doesn't lose
201	 * work that's already in the DB. Read-modify-write on the record's
202	 * `outputExercises` array — safe under CONCURRENCY_LIMIT = 1.
203	 */
204	async function appendOutputExercise(
205	  payload: Payload,
206	  duplicationId: string,
207	  mapping: OutputExerciseMapping,
208	): Promise<void> {
209	  try {
210	    const current = await payload.findByID({
211	      collection: 'lesson-duplications',
212	      id: duplicationId,
213	      depth: 0,
214	      overrideAccess: true,
215	    })
216	    const existing =
217	      (current as unknown as { outputExercises?: OutputExerciseMapping[] }).outputExercises ?? []
218	    await payload.update({
219	      collection: 'lesson-duplications',
220	      id: duplicationId,
221	      data: { outputExercises: [...existing, mapping] } as never,
222	      overrideAccess: true,
223	    })
224	  } catch (err) {
225	    logger.error(
226	      { err, duplicationId, exerciseRef: mapping.sourceExerciseId },
227	      'Failed to append output exercise mapping',
228	    )
229	  }
230	}
231	
232	/** Shape of an exercise from the exercises collection. */
233	type ExerciseDoc = {
234	  id: string
235	  content?: { blocks?: ContentBlock[] }
236	}
237	
238	/** Mapping entry from source exercise to generated output exercise. */
239	interface OutputExerciseMapping {
240	  sourceExerciseId: string
241	  outputExerciseId: string
242	  strategy: DuplicationStrategy
243	}
244	
245	/**
246	 * Create the output lesson (draft) by deep-copying every source-lesson field.
247	 *
248	 * Earlier versions only copied title/slug/status/chapter/course/tenant/locale,
249	 * which dropped fields like accessType, visibleRenderers, blocks, contentStatus,
250	 * and any custom flags the source lesson had. This caused variation outputs to
251	 * be thinner than what the `none` endpoint produces. We now mirror the
252	 * deep-clone behaviour of the `none` path so both routes produce equivalent
253	 * lessons modulo the title suffix and a forced `draft` status.
254	 */
255	async function createOutputLesson(
256	  payload: Payload,
257	  sourceLessonId: string,
258	  level: string,
259	): Promise<string> {
260	  const source = await payload.findByID({
261	    collection: 'lessons',
262	    id: sourceLessonId,
263	    depth: 0,
264	    overrideAccess: true,
265	  })
266	  const sourceData = source as unknown as Record<string, unknown>
267	  // Drop Payload-managed + lineage fields; the rest carries over.
268	  //   slug:           regenerated by formatSlugAsync from the new title.
269	  //   blocks:         references source exercise IDs; rebuilt by the exercise
270	  //                   auto-add hook as new variation exercises are saved.
271	  //   translatedFrom: this is a fresh variation, not a translation copy.
272	  //   createdBy:      set to the duplicating admin via createdByField hook.
273	  const {
274	    id: _id,
275	    createdAt: _c,
276	    updatedAt: _u,
277	    slug: _ignoreSlug,
278	    blocks: _ignoreBlocks,
279	    translatedFrom: _ignoreTranslatedFrom,
280	    createdBy: _ignoreCreatedBy,
281	    ...rest
282	  } = sourceData as Record<string, unknown> & {
283	    id?: unknown
284	    createdAt?: unknown
285	    updatedAt?: unknown
286	    slug?: unknown
287	    blocks?: unknown
288	    translatedFrom?: unknown
289	    createdBy?: unknown
290	  }
291	  void _id
292	  void _c
293	  void _u
294	  void _ignoreSlug
295	  void _ignoreBlocks
296	  void _ignoreTranslatedFrom
297	  void _ignoreCreatedBy
298	  const base = (rest.title as string) ?? 'Lesson'
299	  const newLesson = await payload.create({
300	    collection: 'lessons',
301	    data: {
302	      ...rest,
303	      title: `${base} - Variation (${level})`,
304	      status: 'draft', // never publish a duplicate by default
305	    } as never,
306	    overrideAccess: true,
307	  })
308	  return newLesson.id
309	}
310	
311	/**
312	 * Create a draft exercise in the output lesson with the generated blocks.
313	 * Returns the mapping entry for tracking.
314	 */
315	async function createOutputExercise(
316	  payload: Payload,
317	  result: StrategyResult,
318	  outputLessonId: string,
319	): Promise<OutputExerciseMapping> {
320	  const ex = await payload.create({
321	    collection: 'exercises',
322	    data: {
323	      title: `Variation of ${result.exerciseId}`,
324	      lesson: outputLessonId,
325	      content: { blocks: result.blocks },
326	      status: 'draft',
327	    } as never,
328	    overrideAccess: true,
329	  })
330	  return {
331	    sourceExerciseId: result.exerciseId,
332	    outputExerciseId: ex.id,
333	    strategy: result.strategy,
334	  }
335	}
336	
337	/**
338	 * Process a single exercise through the duplication pipeline.
339	 *
340	 * Steps:
341	 *  1. Run strategy → get new exercise content
342	 *  2. Structural validation → collect failures
343	 *  3. If structural passes AND strategy != 'script' → semantic validation
344	 *  4. On any failure: stream failure to DB, return null
345	 *  5. On all pass: return StrategyResult for the orchestrator to use
346	 */
347	async function processExercise(
348	  exercise: ExerciseDoc,
349	  duplicationId: string,
350	  level: 'none' | 'light' | 'medium' | 'deep',
351	  subject: DuplicationSubject,
352	  payload: Payload,
353	  router?: RouterStrategy,
354	): Promise<StrategyResult | null> {
355	  const exerciseRef = exercise.id
356	
357	  // Step 1: Run strategy
358	  let strategyResult: StrategyResult
359	  try {
360	    strategyResult = await runStrategy(exercise, level, subject, payload, router)
361	  } catch (err) {
362	    const message = err instanceof Error ? err.message : 'Unknown strategy error'
363	    logger.error({ exerciseRef, level, err }, 'Strategy generation failed')
364	    await appendFailure(payload, duplicationId, exerciseRef, 0, GENERATION_FAILURE_CODE, message)
365	    return null
366	  }
367	
368	  // Step 2: Structural validation. Split failures into blocking (drop the
369	  // exercise — renderer would crash) and warnings (admin fills from review
370	  // screen). Warning-only exercises still ship, with TODO placeholders filled
371	  // in for missing hint/solution/fullSolution so the lesson stays renderable.
372	  const structuralFailures: StructuralFailure[] = validateExerciseStructural(strategyResult.blocks)
373	  const blockingFailures = structuralFailures.filter((f) => BLOCKING_FAILURE_CODES.has(f.code))
374	  const warningFailures = structuralFailures.filter((f) => !BLOCKING_FAILURE_CODES.has(f.code))
375	
376	  // Record blocking failures and non-blocking warnings into separate buckets
377	  // so the review UI can show them under different headings.
378	  for (const failure of blockingFailures) {
379	    await appendFailure(
380	      payload,
381	      duplicationId,
382	      exerciseRef,
383	      failure.blockIndex ?? 0,
384	      failure.code,
385	      failure.message,
386	    )
387	  }
388	  for (const warning of warningFailures) {
389	    await appendWarning(
390	      payload,
391	      duplicationId,
392	      exerciseRef,
393	      warning.blockIndex ?? 0,
394	      warning.code,
395	      warning.message,
396	    )
397	  }
398	
399	  if (blockingFailures.length > 0) {
400	    return null
401	  }
402	
403	  // Warning-only path: fill placeholders so the exercise renders. Warnings are
404	  // already recorded so the admin can find the TODOs in the review screen.
405	  if (warningFailures.length > 0) {
406	    strategyResult = {
407	      ...strategyResult,
408	      blocks: fillMissingFieldsWithPlaceholders(strategyResult.blocks),
409	    }
410	  }
411	
412	  // Step 3: Semantic validation. Skipped for:
413	  //  - level=none (deep clone, no AI variation to judge)
414	  //  - script strategy (deterministic, no hallucination risk)
415	  //  - warning-only path (we just inserted "_TODO: hint not provided by AI_"
416	  //    placeholders; a semantic reviewer would flag those as nonsensical and
417	  //    demote an exercise that should ship with a polish-later flag into a
418	  //    hard failure)
419	  if (strategyResult.strategy !== 'script' && level !== 'none' && warningFailures.length === 0) {
420	    const semanticResult = await validateExerciseSemantic(
421	      strategyResult.blocks,
422	      level,
423	      strategyResult.strategy,
424	      payload,
425	    )
426	    if (!semanticResult.ok) {
427	      await appendFailure(
428	        payload,
429	        duplicationId,
430	        exerciseRef,
431	        0,
432	        SEMANTIC_FAILURE_CODE,
433	        `Semantic mismatch: ${semanticResult.reasons.join('; ')}`,
434	      )
435	      return null
436	    }
437	  }
438	
439	  return strategyResult
440	}
441	
442	/**
443	 * Reserve at least this much wall time (ms) at the end of a tick so the
444	 * orchestrator can write its final state to the DB before the platform
445	 * (Vercel) yanks the function. 30s covers a slow Mongo round-trip plus
446	 * Vercel's teardown grace.
447	 */
448	const FINALIZE_BUDGET_MS = 30_000
449	
450	/**
451	 * Default assumed worst-case time for one exercise (pass-1 + pass-2 +
452	 * validators on gemini-3.1-pro-preview). Used to decide whether the next
453	 * exercise can fit before the deadline. Conservative — if the run is
454	 * faster, we just process more per tick.
455	 */
456	const ASSUMED_EXERCISE_DURATION_MS = 300_000
457	
458	export type RunOutcome = 'succeeded' | 'needs_review' | 'failed' | 'in_progress'
459	
460	export interface RunOptions {
461	  /**
462	   * Absolute wall-clock deadline (Date.now() ms). The orchestrator returns
463	   * `in_progress` as soon as the remaining time is too short to fit another
464	   * exercise — leaving the record in `running` so the next cron tick picks
465	   * up where this one left off. Default: no deadline (process all).
466	   */
467	  deadlineMs?: number
468	}
469	
470	/**
471	 * Orchestrate the duplication pipeline for a single LessonDuplications record.
472	 * Resumable: skips exercises already processed in previous ticks and streams
473	 * each new output mapping to the DB as it completes. A Vercel function
474	 * timeout mid-run leaves a clean partial state — the next cron tick continues
475	 * from there.
476	 *
477	 * @param duplicationId  ID of the LessonDuplications record to process
478	 * @param payload        Payload instance
479	<<<<<<< HEAD
480	 * @param router         Optional injectable RouterStrategy for testing
481	=======
482	 * @param options.deadlineMs  Absolute timestamp by which to stop processing
483	>>>>>>> origin/dev
484	 *
485	 * Pre-condition: record has status `pending` (fresh) or `running` (resuming).
486	 * Post-condition (returned outcome):
487	 *  - 'succeeded'   — all picked exercises produced an output, 0 failures
488	 *  - 'needs_review'— all picked exercises terminal, ≥1 failure
489	 *  - 'in_progress' — deadline hit, more work to do, status left as 'running'
490	 *  - 'failed'      — fatal error before any exercises processed
491	 */
492	export async function runDuplicationOrchestrator(
493	  duplicationId: string,
494	  payload: Payload,

497	): Promise<void> {

1	/**
2	 * Lesson Duplication Orchestrator
3	 *
4	 * Drives the duplication pipeline for a single LessonDuplications record:
5	 *  1. K2: Select up to 20 exercises from source lesson (via selectExercisesScaled)
6	 *  2. For each exercise, concurrently (max 3):
7	 *     a. Route to strategy (script or AI) → generates new exercise content
8	 *     b. Run structural validator on the generated content
9	 *     c. If structural passes AND strategy != 'script', run semantic validator
10	 *     d. On any failure, append a failure entry to the LessonDuplications record
11	 *  3. After all exercises settle: set status=succeeded (0 failures) or needs_review (>0 failures)
12	 *
13	 * Failures are streamed live — each failure is written to the DB immediately
14	 * via payload.update(), not accumulated in memory.
15	 */

36	// Concurrency of 1 = process exercises sequentially. Each exercise hits the
37	// LLM twice (creative + deterministic). Gemini's per-minute quota is easily
38	// exceeded at higher concurrency, which triggered "rate limit exceeded"
39	// failures on every exercise in early multi-exercise runs. The variation
40	// service has rate-limit backoff as a safety net, but serializing is the
41	// primary control. Bump this back up only after we've sized the quota AND
42	// replaced appendEntry's read-modify-write with an atomic $push.
43	// `as const` pins the literal type so the compile-time guard below catches

45	export const CONCURRENCY_LIMIT = 1 as const
